divergence: the interstate highway system was never built — road trips take weeks, motels are dynasties, and America is enormous again
story_angle: historical flashback to how another continent coped with the absence centuries ago, framed as a lesson America refuses to learn
headline: Great Transcontinental Caravan Tax Leaves Families in Bureaucratic Gridlock
deck: As car trips become odysseys, the government taxes road warriors for their love of the open road.
article:
The Department of Transcontinental Travel rolled out the Great Transcontinental Caravan Tax, treating family road trips like regulated freight hauls. Travelers must now slog through a 47-page form logging every snack, rest stop, and detour on journeys that once took a week.

Checkpoint agents now demand fuel-efficiency logs from randomly stopped vehicles. Local driver Gary Pickles noted his minivan's mileage dropped after five pounds of gummy bears, forcing a month of data gathering before any forms could be submitted.

A Cultural Enrichment Fee now hits every historical site, scaled by its age and selfie count. Sally Dunes, planning a stop at the Old Pine Cone of Flatwoods, considered legal help after facing a $78 surcharge.

Road travel has shifted from leisure to ledger work, with Pickles summing it up as an audit instead of a vacation while sorting another stack of permits.
author: Harriet Punctual, Senior Lifelong Commuter Correspondent

weather.condition: Traffic Jam Clouds with Bureaucratic Rain

oped.title: The Caravan Tax: A Much-Needed Brake on Wanderlust
oped.author: Horace J. Blunderbuss, Esq., Self-Appointed Guardian of Bureaucratic Efficiency and Proprietor of the 'Endless Horizon' Motel Chain
oped.body: Motorists complaining about the Great Transcontinental Caravan Tax show little appreciation for its benefits. The levy keeps casual drivers off the pavement, sparing highways from needless wear. Those griping about paperwork and delays tend to overpack and wear oversized hats that clog every checkpoint. As owner of Midwest roadside lodgings, I see longer stays boost local trade far more than quick interstate dashes. Adding a hat surcharge would further discourage sightseers and preserve the road for serious travelers.

classifieds.text: 2023 Winnebago, 4,200 miles logged en route to Denver, now parked in Iowa until tax appeal clears. Includes 47 snack receipts and one very tired golden retriever.
Desperate family seeks freelance tax preparer willing to drive paperwork across three state lines. Payment: half our remaining gas money and a lifetime supply of diner pie.
To the in-laws in Oregon: the caravan tax audit delayed us again, we’re still in Wyoming, please stop calling the sheriff. We’ll be there before the kids graduate.
LEGAL NOTICE: All 2026 transcontinental caravans must now carry notarized proof of motel dynasty loyalty. Noncompliance results in immediate bureaucratic gridlock extension.

panels.caption: The Smiths eye the caravan tax scroll: 'Declare every mile and pay the open-road levy upfront.'
Bureaucrat stamps it: 'Add the dynasty motel bond and the gridlock processing fee—six weeks minimum.'
Wife: 'We can't afford the trip.' Husband: 'That's the surcharge for not going.'

joke.setup: Filing the new caravan tax exemption now requires notarized affidavits from every motel dynasty along the route,
joke.punchline: so the Smiths’ 2026 road trip paperwork is being handled by their grandchildren—who still can’t leave the driveway until the 2041 audit clears.
joke.text: Filing the new caravan tax exemption now requires notarized affidavits from every motel dynasty along the route, so the Smiths’ 2026 road trip paperwork is being handled by their grandchildren—who still can’t leave the driveway until the 2041 audit clears.

sponsor_ads.headline: Expedite Your Dynasty: Caravan Tax Arbitrage Services
sponsor_ads.body: Our disbarred accountants reclassify your station wagon as a hereditary motel franchise, slashing the Great Transcontinental levy by forty percent while you idle in gridlock. Families report generational handovers completed before the next toll plaza opens.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Not liable for children born in line.

sponsor_ads.headline: The Eternal Rest Stop: Invest in a Motel Bloodline
sponsor_ads.body: Own shares in a fourth-generation highway dynasty where your descendants collect room keys instead of paying the caravan tax every decade. Three-week stays included; four-week stays require a notarized will update.
sponsor_ads.tagline: America remains enormous—your heirs thank you.

sponsor_ads.headline: Week-Long Caravan Entertainment Kits
sponsor_ads.body: Pre-loaded with 400 hours of static road-trip radio dramas and laminated grievance forms for the bureaucratic gridlock ahead. Batteries not included; neither is forward motion.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Void where interstates were once promised.

sponsor_ads.headline: Mobile Tax Appeal Wagons Now Boarding
sponsor_ads.body: Join our rolling court of auditors that circles the continent twice yearly, converting family gridlock into deductible heritage status. One successful appeal per wagon; survivors receive commemorative pothole maps.

stories.headline: Caravan Tax Forces Logistics Giants to Add Three-Week Surcharges on Cross-State Shipments
stories.deck: Trucking unions demand extra bunks in cabs as routes revert to pre-1950s alignments
stories.article:
New contracts require stops at 1940s motor courts, stretching former day-long hauls into multi-week payroll cycles.

Retailers now preload warehouses half a year ahead, so winter coats land on shelves in July.

stories.headline: Produce Caravans Miss Harvest Windows After New Tax Stalls Cross-Country Hauls
stories.deck: Midwest corn reaches coastal markets only after it has already been replanted
stories.article:
Iowa growers now plant around six-week transit windows to reach New York auctions. Refrigerated trucks sit idle at weigh stations while crews chase extra permits.

Florida lettuce prices jumped when one California convoy paid the full levy, pushing stores toward local greenhouses that cannot scale.

real_world_echo: mirrors the US imposing tariffs over 'forced labour' imports by paralleling the government’s newly implemented tax on caravanning families for 'excessive road usage', which is absurdly high and deeply convoluted
